Slow mornings that reset my whole week

I stopped racing the clock and gave the first hour back to myself. These are the small rituals that actually stuck.

The phone stays in the other room

The first voice I hear shouldn't be a stranger's notification.

I make coffee like it's a ceremony

Ten unhurried minutes are cheaper than any reset day I could plan.

One page of whatever falls out

Journaling isn't a performance. It's just clearing the desk in my head.

Light and air before any screen

I open a window before I open a laptop, every single morning.
